rulings.Case Title: STEWART TITLE OF CALIFORNIA, INC., A CORPORATION VS. PAUL V. GILMORE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 19, 2024 line 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT WESTBAY COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE GROUP, INC., A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. The Court did not locate a separate statement in support of the motion for summary adjudication on file. To the extent the document titled "Separate Statement of Material Facts in Support ofMotion for Summary Judgment or, in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication" was meant to serve as such separate statement contrary to the language on lines 8 and 9 on page 2 of this document ("Motion for Summary Judgment. The undisputed facts and supporting evidence in support of Westbay's Motion for Summary Judgment are as follows:"), this separate statement in not in compliance with CRC 3.1350(b). Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden. Multiple facts are purportedly supported by "Declaration of Andrew Peceimer." There is no such declaration on file. Furthermore, there are triable issues of fact, including, facts 14 and 17. Objections to Gilmore declaration paragraphs 10,11, 17,18,19, 20, and 21 overruled. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO POLICE OFFICERS ASSOCIATION, AN VS. H2A ENTERTAINMENT, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Or Modify Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 19, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT H2A ENTERTAINMENT LLC MOTION TO QUASH OR MODIFY DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS is GRANTED, subject to a protective order. Parties to meet and confer on the form of the protective order. The Court is also inclined to grant the production of the tax filings contained in the files subject to redacting of all information other than the year, the number of employees and the payroll information (if any). Counsel to meet and confer regarding the logistics of redacting the documents to be produced by a third party. The documents to be produced within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Parties to present their proposed protective order and an order reflecting this ruling at the hearing. If parties cannot agree on the form of order prior to the hearing, counsel to appear in person. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SO LING LEE VS. JOHN DOE WAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Fo Plaintiff So Ling Lee To Compel Responses To Plaintiff'S Demand For Documentes, Set One, And Production Without Objection, Etc.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 19, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFF SO LEE NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FO PLAINTIFF SO LING LEE TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S DEMAND FOR DOCUMENTES, SET ONE, AND PRODUCTION WITHOUT OBJECTION, ETC. is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendant shall provide complete verified responses without objections to the requests for production, special interrogatories, and form interrogatories within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $810. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
